payload.message:
A13: Full menu:
- 8 tempo cocktails (CHF 18-20 each)
- The Flip Side signature pair (CHF 34)
- 4 natural wines by the glass (CHF 14-18)
- 2 local beers on tap (CHF 8)
- Espresso and filter coffee (CHF 5-6)
- Non-alcoholic: House-made tonic, ginger beer, Rivella (CHF 6)
- No food. We're not a restaurant. If you're hungry, eat before you come.

A14: Wednesday to Saturday, 7pm to midnight. No earlier - the light has to be right. Tuesday is reserved for private sessions. Sunday-Monday closed. Saturday is our busiest, but Tuesday private sessions have a three-week waitlist already from word of mouth.

A15: Nothing yet. Clean slate. No logo, no Instagram, no website. You're building from scratch. The only thing I know for certain: the typography should feel like a Blue Note record label. Sans serif. Confident. Unhurried.

A16: Schallplatte is intentionally direct. No clever wordplay, no puns. It is what it is. The German feels natural in Bern - we're not trying to be international or trendy. But the visual language can pull from Japanese and American influences (Blue Note, Wong Kar-wai) while the name stays rooted here. Bern is proud. We're not apologizing for being Swiss.
